title: Italian Spinach
subtitle: A quick sautéed spinach with sweet pepper, onion, garlic, and tomato—ready in about 10 minutes.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Garlic paste works great here in place of sliced fresh garlic.
items: ½ cup tomato paste or 1 small tomato, diced
ingredients.callout: Sicilian variation: Add 2 chopped anchovies along with the other ingredients for a deeper, savory flavor.

body.html: A simple Italian-style sautéed spinach cooked with butter, olive oil, sweet red pepper, onion, garlic, and tomato. Everything goes in the pan at once.

items: Heat butter and olive oil in a skillet
Cover and steam over high heat, then simmer until tender

body.html: Tip: Add 2 chopped anchovies to turn this into Sicilian spinach.

slides.title: Heat the Fat
slides.checkboxLabel: Butter and olive oil heated in skillet

items: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp olive oil.
Wait until the butter is melted and the fat is hot.

slides.title: Add Everything & Steam
slides.checkboxLabel: All ingredients in; covered until steam appears

items: Add the spinach, diced red pepper, garlic, diced onion, and tomato paste or diced tomato all at once.
Cover the skillet and cook over high heat until steam appears.

body.html: Sicilian spinach: Add 2 chopped anchovies at this stage.

items: Simmer, covered, until the spinach is fully tender, about 5–6 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning before serving.
